title: Markets Are 95% Sure Of A Rate Cut — But This Italian Bank Says The Fed Will Hold
author: Piero Cingari
description: UniCredit, an Italian bank, stands alone in predicting the Federal Reserve will hold interest rates steady at its December meeting, contrary to market expectations of a 95% probability of a rate cut.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ETF mentioned as market index tracker, no direct positive or negative implications from the article
Demonstrated independent analysis by going against market consensus and providing nuanced economic perspective

title: Communiqué : Orange emprunte sur le marché obligataire 600 millions d’euros
author: N/A
description: Orange, a major telecommunications operator, has issued a 600 million euro bond with a 10.3-year maturity and a 3.250% coupon. This transaction is part of the company's prudent and active balance sheet management strategy.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article presents Orange's bond issuance as a positive move, as it is part of the company's prudent and active balance sheet management strategy. title: Euro zone banks see investment banking boost but outlook stalls shares By Reuters - Investing.com
author: Reuters
description: Euro zone banks reported strong investment banking performance in Q2, but concerns about a tougher outlook weighed on their share prices. Deutsche Bank posted a quarterly loss, while BNP Paribas and Santander saw profits rise, though their retail units showed weakness.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Deutsche Bank reported a quarterly loss related to a provision for a lawsuit, and surprised by axing plans for another buyback and increasing bad loan loss charges.
Deutsche Bank reported a quarterly loss related to a provision for a lawsuit, and surprised by axing plans for another buyback and increasing bad loan loss charges.
Deutsche Bank reported a quarterly loss related to a provision for a lawsuit, and surprised by axing plans for another buyback and increasing bad loan loss charges.
Deutsche Bank reported a quarterly loss related to a provision for a lawsuit, and surprised by axing plans for another buyback and increasing bad loan loss charges.
Deutsche Bank reported a quarterly loss related to a provision for a lawsuit, and surprised by axing plans for another buyback and increasing bad loan loss charges.
BNP Paribas' revenue and profits exceeded analyst expectations, driven by a strong performance in its equities trading and investment banking divisions.
Santander reported a record net profit, but its net interest income came in weaker than expected, though it raised its profitability goals.
UniCredit's profit topped forecasts, and the bank announced it was buying a Belgian digital bank with its own cloud-based IT platform.
